title: Project Brief
description: Shared state document passed between all pipeline stages. Each stage reads what it needs and writes its output section back.

meta.description: Project identity and audience framing — populated in stage 0 (intake).

project_name.description: Human-readable project name used as the document title throughout the pipeline.

industry.description: Primary industry or vertical (e.g. 'specialty coffee', 'fintech', 'fashion retail').

target_audience.description: Concise persona description: demographics, psychographics, key behaviours.

tone_keywords.description: 3–7 adjectives that anchor the brand voice and filter all creative decisions.

creative_direction.description: The creative backbone — concept, story arc, emotional intent, and brand character. Written by the Creative Director stage.

concept.description: Single-sentence creative idea that unifies the entire experience. The elevator pitch for the design.

narrative.description: 2–4 sentence arc describing the story the site tells from entry to conversion.

emotional_tone.description: The dominant feeling the visitor should carry away — distinct from the tone_keywords list.

signature_moment.description: One specific, concrete interaction or visual moment that makes the experience memorable and shareable.

brand_personality.description: The brand rendered as a person: their manner, vocabulary, and what they would never say or do.

ux_structure.description: Information architecture and conversion logic. Written by the UX Strategist stage.

sitemap.description: Ordered list of pages or named sections within a single-page layout.

id.description: Slug used to reference this section elsewhere (e.g. in motion_spec).

purpose.description: The one job this section must accomplish for the visitor.

children.description: Sub-sections or nested pages, same schema.

conversion_flow.description: Plain-English description of the primary path from landing to primary CTA completion.

navigation_pattern.description: Navigation paradigm and behaviour (e.g. 'sticky minimal top-bar collapses to hamburger at 768px').

design_system.description: Visual language tokens. Written by the Visual Design stage; consumed by the component generator.

typography.description: Typeface choices and scale. Use real Google Fonts or Adobe Fonts names.

scale.description: Named sizes in rem. Keys are semantic labels (e.g. 'display', 'h1' … 'caption').

color_palette.description: Complete palette. Every entry must have a hex value and a declared usage role.

spacing_scale.description: Design-token spacing values in rem. Keys are t-shirt sizes or numeric steps.

components.description: List of UI components needed for this project with their purpose and variant notes.

description.description: What it does and any notable variants or states.

motion_spec.description: Animation and scroll behaviour contract. Written by the Motion Designer stage; consumed by the frontend generator.

global_principles.description: Site-wide animation rules that every section must respect.

per_section.description: Per-section motion instructions keyed to sitemap IDs.

section_id.description: Must match a sitemap entry id.

scroll_behavior.description: How scroll position drives visual change (e.g. 'parallax at 0.4× rate', 'pin for 200vh then release').

transitions.description: Enter/exit animation description including direction, opacity, scale, or clip-path.

spatial_3d_spec.description: WebGL / Three.js scene definitions. Written by the 3D Spatial stage; omit scenes if used=false.

used.description: Whether any 3D rendering is required. If false, the 3D stage is skipped.

scenes.description: One entry per distinct 3D scene or canvas element.

description.description: What the scene shows and its narrative purpose.

camera_movement.description: Camera path, rig type, or scroll-driven movement description.

max_triangles.description: Triangle count ceiling for the scene.

target_fps.description: Minimum frame rate on mid-range mobile (e.g. 30).

asset_manifest.description: All media assets required by the project, with generative prompts for AI production.

prompt.description: Full text-to-image prompt passed to the generation model.

style_reference.description: Named photographer, film stock, or visual reference to anchor the style.

usage.description: Where in the layout this image appears.

taste_scores.description: Quality scores from the assets-taste MCP server at each pipeline stage. Scale 0–100 vs reference corpus.

version_history.description: Append-only log. Each pipeline stage appends one entry when it writes to the brief.

stage.description: Name of the pipeline stage that made this write.

summary.description: One sentence describing what changed and why.

notes.description: What was strong, what dragged the score, and the recommended fix.
